comments.author: @bayingblade5532
comments.text: My seventh visit to this beautiful country coming up in August. I have experienced Budapest numerous times, Visigrad, Esztergom, Szentendre and the Balaton.  There are no huge mountains, no great outcrops, no bottomless lakes........just beauty around every corner.....culture, history and an atmosphere like nowhere else. I have discovered fabulous food and wine and grow to love this place a little more every time I visit. This year, I will be spending a few days in Tokaj, in a hotel in the middle of a vineyard with its own winery. I have a feeling that my love of Hungary will only grow further!

replies.author: @georgebelakemeny9221
replies.text: I'm sure you will love it better and better. have a good time it Tokaj. Be careful if you go to a vine cellar because of the different temperature between the cellar and the ground above you wil feel drank when you come up to the warm surface even if you just had a couple glasses of vine.

comments.author: @963ag
comments.text: Both of my parents were Hungarian refugees, I was born and raised in the United States and have visited Hungary as a child, during the 1970s. I wish that I could visit again, but health and finances prevent me. My father was from Sopron and also lived in Kapuvar, and my mother was born in Dunabogdany. I can read, speak and understand Hungarian, but don't write it.

replies.author: @mariar6806
replies.text: I am from Fertoszentmiklos which is near Kapuvar. Came to Australia in 1956 as a child and only visited last year (2025). I tear up watching anything about this beautidful country, the people and the food. I am too old to go again and this hurts my soul. I long to go back. I have so many memories burned into my soul about this land and its people.

comments.author: @ildiko1vt
comments.text:
My home country as well. Left after the Hungarian Revolution, which this video does not reflect upon. 
There are many errors, as with St Stephen's Basilica mixed in with the Fisherman's Bastion, which are on opposite sides of the river. Wish there was a more continuous and through description of Budapest, instead of jumping from covering Budapest to the other cities that are nowhere near it. Disorganized for sure.
(I wish the speaker would pronounce the names of the towns correctly.)

replies.author: @WorldTravelDoc
replies.text: Thank you for sharing your personal history and for the feedback regarding our pronunciation, we truly appreciate your perspective! 🇭🇺

comments.text: This is actually a very poor documentary about Hungary.  Getting sick and tired of foreigners and now AI bastardizing the Hungarian language. Ignored north eastern Hungary such as the first wine growing region in Europe, that being Tokay. Ignored Miskolc as well. I wish I could like this video but I cannot. It royally sucks ass.

replies.text: What a wonderful memory to share! 🇭🇺 Hungary really has a way of exceeding expectations. Once you've experienced its history, scenery, and atmosphere, it's easy to start planning the next trip before you've even left. Which place stole your heart the most?
